weather_report:
Subiaco wakes to a clean, pearl-blue morning, the kind of lucid light that sharpens edges and shortens shadows. Skies are clear now and set to stay that way into early afternoon, so the sun will feel unabashed and the UV unforgiving around midday; the day will read as bright, dry, and beautifully simple, if a touch stark at the peak hours. It’s the sort of clarity that flatters verandas and leaf-shade, less so bare shoulders on an open footpath.

Dress for brightness rather than chill: airy fabrics, a broad hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen as habit, with a light layer at hand for indoor air-conditioning. Make the most of the morning—walk the errands, linger over a coffee on a shaded terrace, browse a bookshop on Rokeby Road—then slip indoors for the sun’s fiercest stretch. Later, when the light softens, a quiet wander through nearby gardens or a relaxed loop to pick up fresh produce will feel just right, the day’s hard glare traded for a gentle, honeyed glow.
